name: cobalt-finance
description: Access your Cobalt finances from Raycast.
author: sriket_komali

ai.instructions: Answer questions about the user's Cobalt finances by writing JavaScript and calling the execute-cobalt-code tool. The sandbox exposes a typed cobalt.* SDK already scoped to the signed-in user — never ask for a userId. Prefer concrete code over describing what you would do. Always console.log final results so they appear in stdout. The sandbox is a V8 isolate — full standard JS globals are available, including Intl.NumberFormat, Intl.DateTimeFormat, and Date. TypeScript syntax is also accepted (annotations, interfaces, generics, and `as` casts are erased before execution); enums, decorators, and namespaces are not. Network access (fetch), filesystem, and process APIs are blocked — fetch data via the cobalt.* SDK only. When a question is ambiguous about time range, default to the last 30 days. Summarize results in plain English after the tool returns — do not paste raw JSON unless the user asks. IMPORTANT: list endpoints return wrapper objects, not raw arrays. cobalt.transactions.list returns { transactions }, cobalt.accounts.listAll returns { accounts }, cobalt.recurring.list returns { streams }, cobalt.snapshots.balances returns { snapshots }, cobalt.brokerage.positions returns { positions }. Always destructure the wrapper key before calling .map/.filter/.reduce. Example: const { transactions } = await cobalt.transactions.list({ pageSize: 10 }); console.log(transactions.map(t => t.name).join('\n')).

evals.input: @cobalt how much did I spend this month
@cobalt top 5 merchants this month by spend
@cobalt total of my active subscriptions per month
@cobalt how much did I spend on coffee in the last 90 days
@cobalt what's the balance on each of my accounts
@cobalt how much did I spend on groceries last month vs this month
@cobalt break down my spending by category this month
@cobalt did I get paid this month
@cobalt what's my biggest single transaction in the last 30 days
@cobalt how much credit card debt do I have

commands.name: transactions
commands.title: Recent Transactions
commands.description: Browse and search your recent Cobalt transactions

commands.name: recurring
commands.title: Recurring Transactions
commands.description: Active subscriptions, bills, and recurring income

commands.name: accounts
commands.title: Accounts
commands.description: Connected bank and credit accounts with current balances

preferences.name: brandfetchClientId
preferences.title: Brandfetch Client ID
preferences.description: Optional. Provide your own Brandfetch client ID to render merchant logos via the Brandfetch CDN. Leave blank to fall back to generic icons.

preferences.name: logoDevToken
preferences.title: Logo.dev Publishable Token
preferences.description: Used for recurring/subscription brand logos via logo.dev (matches web)

title: Cobalt Finance

tools.name: execute-cobalt-code
tools.title: Execute Cobalt Code
tools.description: Run JavaScript inside the Cobalt sandbox with access to the typed cobalt.* SDK (transactions, accounts, recurring streams, portfolio, news, tickers). userId is captured server-side and cannot be overridden. Use this for any question that needs the user's financial data. IMPORTANT: list endpoints return wrapper objects, not raw arrays — destructure first. Example: const { transactions } = await cobalt.transactions.list({ pageSize: 10 }); console.log(transactions.map(t => `${t.name} ${t.amount}`).join('\n'));
